methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Gladia is a Series A-stage AI & Software company founded by Jean-Louis Quéguiner that has raised $20M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir, PLTR), spanning enterprise AI and frontier-tech segments.

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage and sector. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying $40M. Peer-median analysis across three same-stage, same-sector comps—Liquid AI ($2.4B), Field AI ($2.0B), and Twelve Labs ($250M)—yields a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $250M to $2.4B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in commercialization progress and capital efficiency within early-stage AI.

These methods triangulate to a range of $34M to $2.3B, with a median of $1.0B. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound conservatively, while peer comps dominate the upper range. Key uncertainties include heterogeneity in peer stage definitions (some comps may be further along in revenue or product maturity), Gladia's specific traction relative to the peer set, and the stability of recent AI-sector valuations. The wide range reflects genuine ambiguity; further diligence on revenue, user metrics, and burn would sharpen conviction significantly.
